questions: cooperative ar company er difference ki?
answers: A cooperative is a business owned and democratically controlled by its members who share the profits and benefits, operating on principles of mutual help and equality.
Cooperative holo ekta business model jekhane members nijerai owner. Shobai mile decision ney ar profit share kore. Milk cooperative, credit union eirokom example.
Types of cooperatives include consumer co-ops, worker co-ops, producer co-ops, credit unions, and housing co-ops, each serving different member needs.
Unlike a regular company owned by shareholders, a cooperative is owned by its members who each get one vote regardless of investment, focusing on member benefit over profit maximization.
